title: Changes to routine childhood immunisation programme: 2025 and 2026
body: "By the end of this session, learners should be able to describe:​  • what changes are being made to the childhood immunisation schedule in 2025 and 2026 and why they are being made  • how the changes to the schedule at 12 and 16 weeks should be delivered  • which children are eligible for the new 18-month hexavalent vaccine appointment  • when the second dose of MMR vaccine should be given  • how the changes will affect children on the selective neonatal hepatitis B pathway  • where to find further information about the changes  Find PowerPoint version of slide set ‘Changes to routine childhood immunisation programme in 2025 and 2026’  "
